CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Set For Trial On Nov-09-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aileen M. McGrath, Esq. and Ronald H. Lee, Esq., Deputy City Attorneys, T:415.554.4691, for defendant/moving party CCSF;
Amy L. Silverstein, Esq. and Robert T. Petraglia, Esq. of SILVERSTEIN & POMERANTZ LLP, T:415.593.3502, for plaintiff/opposing party;

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Jose Cisneros' motion to strike paragraphs 41-60 and 89-96 from the complaint filed by plaintiff Square, Inc. is granted in its entirety. Paragraphs 41-60 and 89-96 allege factual and legal bases for a tax refund that were not fairly described in Square's Claim for Refund. The Claim for Refund alleges that Square is entitled to a refund for three reasons: 1) "for gross receipts taxes paid to other jurisdictions on the same gross receipts taxed by the City," 2) because "the amount originally reported included payments collected by third parties" and 3) "its business activities . . . were improperly classified for purposes of determining its liability." Paragraphs 41-60 and 89-96 of the complaint allege five distinct factual and legal grounds for a refund that were not explicitly or implicitly included in any of the three grounds alleged in the Claim for Refund and thus Square may not assert those grounds in this lawsuit.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2019 Continued To Dec-09-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
